list.name: Changes
list.comment: feat: merge history + reference list into unified Fluent-style reference manager Summary Replaced the separate History and Reference List components with a single, unified reference manager that behaves consistently, stores entries persistently, and aligns with Harvard referencing workflows. Key changes • Removed the History feature entirely (UI, JS logic, localStorage usage). • Introduced a single reference list with: ◦ alphabetical ordering ◦ click‑to‑restore behaviour ◦ delete (×) control per entry ◦ persistent storage via `referenceist ◦ no numbering (Harvard‑compliant) • Updated `rendereferenceList() to use a flex layout and prevent the × from appearing inside the reference text. • Updated “Add to List”, “Copy All”, and “Download” logic to use the unified list. • Cleaned up DOMContentLoaded initialisation to load only `referenceist. • Removed all history‑related CSS and replaced with Fluent‑style reference list styling. • Added new Fluent Light CSS for .reference-item, `.reference-text and .reference-delete. • Improved UI consistency across the app to match Fluent Light design. • Updated WebsiteFormatter to auto‑insert today’s date when “Date of access” is left blank. • Updated field label to “Date of access (current date unless changed)”. Result The referencing tool now has a cleaner, more intuitive workflow with a single, alphabetised reference list that feels native, consistent, and easier for users to manage. The UI is simpler, the codebase is cleaner, and the overall experience is more Fluent and professional.

task.summary: Initial commit of Harvard referencing tool Flask version

feat: merge history + reference list into unified Fluent-style reference manager Summary Replaced the separate History and Reference List components with a single, unified reference manager that behaves consistently, stores entries persistently, and aligns with Harvard referencing workflows. Key changes • Removed the History feature entirely (UI, JS logic, localStorage usage). • Introduced a single reference list with: ◦ alphabetical ordering ◦ click‑to‑restore behaviour ◦ delete (×) control per entry ◦ persistent storage via `referenceist ◦ no numbering (Harvard‑compliant) • Updated `rendereferenceList() to use a flex layout and prevent the × from appearing inside the reference text. • Updated “Add to List”, “Copy All”, and “Download” logic to use the unified list. • Cleaned up DOMContentLoaded initialisation to load only `referenceist. • Removed all history‑related CSS and replaced with Fluent‑style reference list styling. • Added new Fluent Light CSS for .reference-item, `.reference-text and .reference-delete. • Improved UI consistency across the app to match Fluent Light design. • Updated WebsiteFormatter to auto‑insert today’s date when “Date of access” is left blank. • Updated field label to “Date of access (current date unless changed)”. Result The referencing tool now has a cleaner, more intuitive workflow with a single, alphabetised reference list that feels native, consistent, and easier for users to manage. The UI is simpler, the codebase is cleaner, and the overall experience is more Fluent and professional.

MESSAGE.value: Initial commit of Harvard referencing tool Flask version

feat: merge history + reference list into unified Fluent-style reference manager Summary Replaced the separate History and Reference List components with a single, unified reference manager that behaves consistently, stores entries persistently, and aligns with Harvard referencing workflows. Key changes • Removed the History feature entirely (UI, JS logic, localStorage usage). • Introduced a single reference list with: ◦ alphabetical ordering ◦ click‑to‑restore behaviour ◦ delete (×) control per entry ◦ persistent storage via `referenceist ◦ no numbering (Harvard‑compliant) • Updated `rendereferenceList() to use a flex layout and prevent the × from appearing inside the reference text. • Updated “Add to List”, “Copy All”, and “Download” logic to use the unified list. • Cleaned up DOMContentLoaded initialisation to load only `referenceist. • Removed all history‑related CSS and replaced with Fluent‑style reference list styling. • Added new Fluent Light CSS for .reference-item, `.reference-text and .reference-delete. • Improved UI consistency across the app to match Fluent Light design. • Updated WebsiteFormatter to auto‑insert today’s date when “Date of access” is left blank. • Updated field label to “Date of access (current date unless changed)”. Result The referencing tool now has a cleaner, more intuitive workflow with a single, alphabetised reference list that feels native, consistent, and easier for users to manage. The UI is simpler, the codebase is cleaner, and the overall experience is more Fluent and professional.

option.name: LAST_COMMIT_MESSAGE
option.value: feat: merge history + reference list into unified Fluent-style reference manager Summary Replaced the separate History and Reference List components with a single, unified reference manager that behaves consistently, stores entries persistently, and aligns with Harvard referencing workflows. Key changes • Removed the History feature entirely (UI, JS logic, localStorage usage). • Introduced a single reference list with: ◦ alphabetical ordering ◦ click‑to‑restore behaviour ◦ delete (×) control per entry ◦ persistent storage via `referenceist ◦ no numbering (Harvard‑compliant) • Updated `rendereferenceList() to use a flex layout and prevent the × from appearing inside the reference text. • Updated “Add to List”, “Copy All”, and “Download” logic to use the unified list. • Cleaned up DOMContentLoaded initialisation to load only `referenceist. • Removed all history‑related CSS and replaced with Fluent‑style reference list styling. • Added new Fluent Light CSS for .reference-item, `.reference-text and .reference-delete. • Improved UI consistency across the app to match Fluent Light design. • Updated WebsiteFormatter to auto‑insert today’s date when “Date of access” is left blank. • Updated field label to “Date of access (current date unless changed)”. Result The referencing tool now has a cleaner, more intuitive workflow with a single, alphabetised reference list that feels native, consistent, and easier for users to manage. The UI is simpler, the codebase is cleaner, and the overall experience is more Fluent and professional.
